race.name: Rad am Ring
race.tagline: Race the Green Hell. 12,000 cyclists on the world's most famous motorsport circuit — the Nurburgring Nordschleife.

vitals.registration: Online via radamring.de. 24h slots sell out within 1 hour. Public races and touring still available.
vitals.entry_fee: Varies by discipline. Public races estimated EUR50-120.
vitals.feed_zones: Feed zones on course. Full mechanical and medical support from motorsport-grade infrastructure.
vitals.cutoff_time: Varies by discipline. 24h race runs midnight to midnight.
route_options: Public Race 25 km (1 lap): 560m elevation — entry level
Public Race 75 km (3 laps): 1,680m elevation — intermediate
Public Race 150 km (6 laps): 3,360m elevation — ambitious
24h Road Race: as many 25.9 km laps as possible in 24 hours (solo or teams of 2/4/8)
22 km Time Trial: Friday evening on GP circuit
Gravel Race 25/50/100 km: on and off the Nordschleife (added 2025)

climate.description: Late July in the Eifel region at 600m elevation delivers mild summer temperatures averaging 22C highs and 12C lows. The Nurburgring's microclimate is notoriously variable — the Eifel's proximity to Atlantic weather systems means rain can appear without warning. July averages 17 rainy days. Morning fog and humidity (76% average) are common, particularly dangerous on the Nordschleife's fast descents where grip disappears.
challenges: Eifel weather volatility: rain can arrive mid-lap with zero warning
Morning fog on the Nordschleife — reduced visibility through forest sections
Wet tarmac on racing circuit at high-speed descent points (Fuchsrohre)
Cool overnight temperatures for 24h participants: can drop to 8-10C

terrain.surface: Purpose-built racing circuit tarmac in immaculate condition. The Nordschleife surface provides excellent grip in dry conditions but becomes treacherous when wet. No public roads, no traffic, no gravel. Pure racetrack.
features: Nordschleife: 20.8 km, 73 bends, 500+ meters of elevation change — the legendary Green Hell
Grand Prix circuit: 5.1 km modern F1-grade track completing the full lap
Hohe Acht climb: the roof of the course at 17% gradient, where even road cyclists understand the Green Hell nickname
Fuchsrohre descent: speeds up to 100 km/h have been measured on bicycles
84 turns per full lap combining both circuits — relentless direction changes
Elevation range: 330m to 625m within each lap — constant undulation, no flat sections

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: A relentless, undulating closed circuit combining the legendary Nordschleife (20.8 km) with the modern Grand Prix circuit (5.1 km). No flat sections exist — you are always either climbing or descending through 84 turns per lap. The forest-enclosed Nordschleife feels otherworldly, while the GP circuit provides the F1 arena start/finish spectacle. Perfect tarmac throughout, but the elevation profile punishes anyone who starts too fast.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Hohe Acht climb at 17% gradient — the roof of the Nordschleife where every lap extracts its toll. Combined with the relentless undulation of the full circuit (560m elevation per lap), there is no recovery. The Green Hell simply grinds you down.

logistics.airport: Cologne-Bonn (CGN) 70 km / 1 hour. Frankfurt (FRA) 160 km / 1.5 hours. Dusseldorf (DUS) 130 km / 1.5 hours. Rental car required — Nurburg has no rail connection.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book early. Nurburg village is tiny with limited capacity. Adenau (5 km) has more options. Mayen, Bad Munstereifel, and Kelberg offer budget alternatives 20-30 km away. 24h participants can use paddock camping plots (book with race entry). Consider Cologne as a base with 1-hour commute if local lodging is sold out.
logistics.food: Limited dining in Nurburg village. Adenau has restaurants and supermarkets. The Nurburgring complex has catering during the event weekend. Stock race nutrition in advance — this is rural Eifel, not a city. 24h teams should plan self-sufficient catering from paddock area.

history.origin_story: Rad am Ring began in 2003 as a small 24-hour road bike race with approximately 70 participants on the Nurburgring. The concept was simple but audacious: use the world's most famous motorsport circuit for bicycle racing. The Nurburgring's Nordschleife, built in 1927 and nicknamed the 'Green Hell' by Jackie Stewart, had never hosted organized cycling competition at scale. The event grew steadily, adding public races, mountain bike disciplines, and touring options. In 2016, a professional road race — the Rudi Altig Race — was added to honor German cyclist Rudi Altig, who won the Road World Championship at the Nurburgring in 1966. Altig died weeks before the inaugural pro race, adding poignant significance. Paul Voss won the first edition in a 70 km solo breakaway. The pro race ran as a UCI Europe Tour 1.1 event before moving to the German Bundesliga. By 2025, the event had grown to nearly 12,000 participants, with 24h race slots selling out in under one hour. In 2025, a gravel race was added, and the 2026 edition will host the German Gravel Championship.
notable_moments: 2003: First edition with approximately 70 riders in the 24h race
2016: Inaugural Rudi Altig Race (pro road race, UCI 1.1). Paul Voss wins with 70 km solo breakaway. Emanuel Buchmann and Davide Rebellin in the field
1966: Rudi Altig wins Road World Championship at the Nurburgring — the event's spiritual origin
2025: Gravel race added for the first time, 550 participants in inaugural edition
2026: German Gravel Championship awarded to Rad am Ring
history.reputation: Germany's premier motorsport-circuit cycling festival. The 24h race is the crown jewel — a unique endurance challenge on sacred motorsport ground. Growing rapidly with a passionate community and institutional support from Nurburgring 1927 GmbH.

biased_opinion.verdict: The Green Hell on Two Wheels
biased_opinion.summary: Rad am Ring does something no other cycling event can: it puts you on the Nurburgring Nordschleife. The same 20.8 km of relentless, forest-enclosed circuit where motorsport legends were forged since 1927. Where Rudi Altig won the Road World Championship in 1966. Where Jackie Stewart coined the name 'Green Hell.' Nearly 12,000 cyclists now invade this sacred motorsport ground every July, racing through 84 turns per lap, surviving the 17% Hohe Acht climb, and hitting 100 km/h on the Fuchsrohre descent. The 24h race adds a surreal dimension — riding the Nordschleife at 3 AM through fog with only a headlamp. This is not gravel, this is not a gran fondo — this is road cycling on the most storied racing circuit on Earth, and the experience is absolutely singular.
strengths: The Nurburgring Nordschleife: literally no other cycling event on Earth offers this venue
World-class motorsport infrastructure applied to cycling: F1 pit lane, closed circuit, medical teams
12,000 participants and growing — one of Germany's biggest cycling festivals
Multi-discipline format: 24h, public races, time trial, gravel, touring — something for everyone
Perfect racetrack surface with 560m climbing per lap and gradients up to 17%
Rich history: Rudi Altig's 1966 World Championship, pro race legacy, event running since 2003
weaknesses: Lap-based racing on a circuit can feel repetitive compared to point-to-point events
No meaningful altitude challenge — the Eifel sits at only 600m
Primarily German/regional field — lacks the international depth of top European sportives
Tiny village of Nurburg has limited accommodation — books out fast for the weekend
Eifel weather is unpredictable — rain on the Nordschleife's fast descents is genuinely dangerous
Not a gravel event — this is pure road cycling on tarmac (gravel discipline added 2025 but secondary)
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you have ever watched a Formula 1 race, a Nurburgring 24 Hours, or read about the Green Hell and thought 'I want to experience that' — Rad am Ring is how you do it on a bicycle. The venue elevates what would otherwise be a solid German sportive into something genuinely unique. The 24h race is the bucket-list version. The experience score earns a perfect 5 because nothing else compares. For German and European cyclists, this should be on the short list. For international travelers, pair it with a European cycling trip — the Nurburgring alone justifies the detour.

final_verdict.one_liner: Race the Green Hell. 12,000 cyclists on the world's most famous motorsport circuit — the Nurburgring Nordschleife.
final_verdict.should_you_race: If you have any connection to motorsport culture, this is a mandatory pilgrimage on two wheels. The 24h race is the crown jewel — 24 hours of closed-circuit racing through the Nordschleife, including surreal night laps through forest fog. The public races (25/75/150 km) offer the venue experience in a single day. For German and European cyclists, this is one of the continent's most unique events. For international visitors, combine with a broader European cycling trip.
final_verdict.alternatives: For other iconic European circuit events: Tour de Okinawa (road racing on closed circuit, Japan). For German cycling festivals: 3Rides Aachen (gravel, nearby), Cyclassics Hamburg (road). For 24h endurance cycling: 24h Montabaur, 24h Duisburg. For the ultimate European sportive experience: L'Etape du Tour, Maratona dles Dolomites.

videos.title: Rad am Ring 24h Solo: Meine offene Rechnung mit der Grünen Hölle
videos.description:
Letztes Jahr bin ich an der Grünen Hölle gescheitert. Dieses Jahr bin ich zurückgekehrt, um eine offene Rechnung zu begleichen. In diesem Video nehme ich euch mit auf meine emotionale Achterbahnfahrt als Solo-Starter beim 24h-Rennen von Rad am Ring 2025.

Erlebt mit mir den euphorischen Start, den brutalen Kampf gegen die Müdigkeit in der Nacht und die schwere Entscheidung, die am Ende alles verändert hat. Das ist der ungeschönte Bericht meines Versuchs, die 8848 Höhenmeter des Mount Everest auf der Nordschleife zu bezwingen. Ist das die Revanche, die ich mir erhofft habe? Findet es heraus.

💬 Was war eure bisher größte und härteste Herausforderung auf dem Rad und wie seid ihr damit umgegangen?
Schreib's mir in die Kommentare. Ich bin gespannt auf eure Geschichten!

🏁 Alle Fakten zum Rennen: 
・Event: Rad am Ring 24h-Rennen 2025
・Strecke: Nürburgring (Nordschleife + GP-Strecke)
・Modus: Einzelfahrer / Solo
・Mehr Infos zum Event: https://www.radamring.de

🏔️ Mein Ergebnis:
・Runden: 12
・Distanz: 312,5 km
・Höhenmeter: 6528 hm
・Reine Sattelzeit: ca. 14 Stunden

🔔 Kanal abonnieren & kein Video mehr verpassen: https://www.youtube.com/user/h0bsc0tch?sub_confirmation=1

videos.title: SWR Fernsehen: Rad am Ring - Grüne Hölle auf zwei Rädern mit der 20 Zoll Bagasch
videos.description: Mit dem Fahrrad über den Nürburgring: Das beweisen in diesem Jahr 10.000 Teilnehmer:innen bei "Rad am Ring". Höhepunkt ist das 24-Stunden-Radrennen über die weltberühmte Nordschleife und den Grand Prix Kurs in der Eifel. Dabei erreichen die Räder bergab mehr als 100 Stundenkilometer Geschwindigkeit. Die Kamera begleitet eine Fahrergruppe, die sich der Herausforderung der grünen Hölle auf dem Klapprad stellt, ein Frauenteam, dass für den guten Zweck fährt und den ältesten Teilnehmer des Wochenendes. Er will mit über 80 seine Topplatzierung aus dem vergangenen Jahr verbessern. Neben der Rennstrecke ist vom Einmannzelt bis zum perfekt ausgestattetem Camping-Paradies alles zu finden. Radsportler:innen aus Deutschland und ganz Europa kommen zum jährlichen Event in die Eifel. Manche, um mit E-Bike und Fahrradkorb über den wohl berühmtesten Asphalt Deutschlands zu radeln, andere für den Geschwindigkeitsrausch in der Fuchsröhre und die völlige Verausgabung beim 24-Stunden-Solo-Renneinsatz.
videos.curation_reason: Recent 2024 comprehensive documentary covering unique race aspects including extreme challenges like folding bike participation.

videos.title: Rad am Ring 2025 – Onboard-Video vom Jedermannrennen 75km
videos.description:
Willkommen zu meinem Onboard-Video vom Rad am Ring 2025! 

Ich war beim 75 km Jedermannrennen (3 Runden auf der Nordschleife + Grand-Prix-Strecke) dabei und habe die Kamera mitlaufen lassen.

🎥 Im Video siehst du knapp über zwei Runden – los geht’s mit der Abfahrt in die Grüne Hölle. Danach folgt Rennatmosphäre pur: enge Kurven, steile Rampen und rasante Abfahrten.
Der Akku war leider kurz nach Beginn von Runde 3 leer – im Nachhinein halb so schlimm, denn da zwangen mich beidseitige Krämpfe zu einer kurzen Pause. 
Ich hatte deutlich zu wenig getrunken und das hat sich ziemlich schnell gerächt.

📊 Rundenzeiten bei knapp über 100 kg Körpergewicht:
• Runde 1:  0: 56: 49
• Runde 2:  1: 07: 11
• Runde 3:  1: 24: 59 (15 min Pause wg. krämpfen)

🚴‍♂️ Gesamtstrecke: ca. 75 km, 1.500 hm
🔋 Gefilmt: ca. 50 km (2 Runden)
📍 Highlights: Fuchsröhre, Hohe Acht, Kesselchen, Karussell – und natürlich die Zielgerade

💡 Bonus-Fact: Mit meinem Gewicht war die Schwerkraft mein größter Verbündeter – bergab lief’s richtig gut. Nur bergauf… reden wir nicht drüber 😅

Strava: https://www.strava.com/activities/15166011682

Wenn dir das Video gefällt, freue ich mich über ein Like oder Kommentar!
Warst du auch am Start? Dann schreib’s gerne unten rein 👇

#RadAmRing #RadAmRing2025 #nürburgring #nuerburgring #nordschleife  #Jedermannrennen #cyclingpov #factorbikes #ostrovam #osmoaction5  #bikerace #onboardvideo  #onboard  #radsport #fahrrad #rennstrecke #circuit #grünehölle #greenhell #cycling #RoadBikeLife #strava

videos.title: Rad am Ring 2015 | 24h-Radrennen auf dem legendären Nürburgring | 24h-race in the Eifel
videos.description:
***WITH ENGLISH SUBTITLES*** Rad am Ring 2015 in der Eifel. Über 4000 Hobbyradsportler beim 24 Stunden Radrennen am Nürburgring. Rund 26 Kilometer misst eine Runde. Knapp 80 Kurven und über 500 Höhenmeter sind zu bewältigen: Das Team Alpecin stellt 2 aktuelle Jedermänner-Teams und zwei All Star Teams. Ausserdem noch eine weitere Damen-Equipe im Liv Plantur Outfit und einen Allein-Fahrer. 


++++++++++

Jetzt auf Facebook folgen: www.facebook.com/teamalpecin

Alpecin Cycling TV ist der offizielle deutsche Youtube Kanal vom Team Alpecin. Auf Alpecin Cycling TV gibt es regelmäßig die neuesten Informationen und interessante Hintergründe rund um den Rennradsport. Hier erfahrt ihr mehr über die Profis von Giant Alpecin, die Jedermänner vom Team Alpecin sowie Tipps und Tricks zu Technik und Equipment.
